title: astragalus longifolius
intro: Astragalus longifolius, also known as the long-leaved milk vetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family that is native to China. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and immunomodulatory effects, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant has been used to support overall health and well-being, with reported benefits including improved cardiovascular function, enhanced immune response, and reduced risk of infections. Astragalus longifolius can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or supplement, and is often combined with other herbs to create a tonic that promotes balance and vitality in the body.
